Off Calendar Sep-25-2017 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-11-2017 Continued To Sep-25-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jan-27-2017 Vacated

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition For Probate Of Will And For Letters Testamentary
Off Calendar Mar-06-2017 Granted Per Written Order

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition For Probate Of Will And For Letters Testamentary
Off Calendar Feb-08-2017 Continued To Mar-06-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Approval Of Will Contest Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Jan-30-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Darin Leviloff for and with Chris Anello and Michelle Anello Forest (contestants/moving parties).
Julia Butler for and with Barry Anello and Cathy Anello.

Ms. Butler stated that the loans have been paid, that the loans were liens against the property and that they were paid out of escrow. Ms. Butler also stated that she was here to get the settlement agreement approved, that the will contest will be dismissed, that they will proceed with the petition for probate and that the receipts for the blocked accounts have been filed. The Court informed Counsel that it will not approve the expense reimbursement and the proposed reserve until a petition for final distribution is heard. The Court granted the Petition for Order Approving Settlement Agreement (subject to review and modification of the proposed order).

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: L. Maloney. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Mediation
Off Calendar Jan-09-2017 Off Calendar

Ex Parte Petition For Order Expanding Special Administrators Special Powers To Allow For, Authorize, And Confirm Pending Sale Of Estates 50 Percent Interest In Real Property And Memorandum Re: Same
Off Calendar Dec-15-2016 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Oct-19-2016 Continued To May-08-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Julia Butler for and with Barry Anello & Catherine Anello. Darren Leviloff for (contestants) Chris Anello & Michelle Anello Forrest.

The Court asked for counsels position on mediation. Mr. Leviloff stated that they will agree to mediation or trial, that they are advocating for trial, that he spoke with Ms. Butler this morning regarding mediation, that they do not want it used as a delay tactic, and that they agree to mediate but also want trial set for April 2017. Ms. Butler stated that they just want to mediate, that it can be done quickly, that they can talk prior to the mediation date and try to resolve the matters and that they are prepared to negotiate. The Court stated that it can set a deadline and status for mediation but is also inclined to set a trial date. Discussion was held as to available dates. Mr. Leviloff stated that there is an issue of the validity/interpretation of the will and that if trial goes forward they want that issue included. Ms. Butler stated that it was not part of the will contest and that interpretation has not been brought up. The Court stated that what was prayed for in the petition is what is being set for trial and that they can file amendments for the trial court. Ms. Butler stated that regarding the request for special administration their request for general powers was denied, that special powers such as getting work done to get the house ready for sale were granted, that an outstanding issue is the personal possessions in the house that had been valued at $8000.00 which was high, that they have had an estate sale person look at the possessions and says that the value is $2000.00, that they would like amended letters to include disposal of the items and waiving appraisal of those items. Mr. Leviloff stated that they have no objections, that regarding the nostalgic items they have discussed allowing the heirs being able to come to the house and request specific items and that they want Cathy present during this time. The Court stated that it will grant the additional power and asked counsel to submit a revised order. Status on mediation was set for January 9, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The matter was referred to the department of the Presiding Judge (206) to be set for trial on May 8, 2017 , (order for the same signed in open court and mailed to the parties).
Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Oct-17-2016 Continued To Oct-19-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF SPECIAL ADMINISTRATION WITH GENERAL POWERS
Off Calendar Sep-30-2016 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jul-25-2016 Continued To Oct-17-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gina Travaglio for (petitioners) Catherine Anello & Barry Anello. Julia Butler (substituting in) for (petitioners) Catherine Anello & Barry Anello. Darin Leviloff for (contestants) Chris Anello & Michelle Anello.

Ms. Butler reported that she was retained late last week, that she needs to reply to the contest, that her clients have requested an evaluation, that they were propounded with discovery and need a 30 day continuance. The Court asked if counsel would like to set a mediation deadline for November or December. Mr. Leviloff stated that they had some informal discussions that went nowhere and that his clients are amenable to mediation. Ms. Butler stated that she does not have authority at this time. Ms. Travaglio stated that what was offered is so far from what the clients are asking for. The Court stated that it needs a summons on the will contest and that it does not want this matter to languish. Mr. Leviloff stated that they are waiting for a response on the discovery. The Court continued the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary to October 17, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and stated that it will either set a trial date in 2017 or a mediation deadline at this hearing.

Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jun-27-2016 Continued To Jul-25-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gina Travaglio for and with Catherine Anello & Barry Anello. Darin Leviloff for and with (objectors) Chris Anello & Michelle Anello.

Ms. Travaglio reported that objections were filed, that she spoke with counsel and provided him with a witness list, a video of Ms. Anello where she speaks of her will, a declaration from her grand niece and a certificate of mental competency and stated that the main beneficiary is Ms. Anello's 84 year old son who would like to sell the property as there is not much money left. Mr. Leviloff stated that they filed objections, that he spoke with Ms. Travaglio on Friday, that he received documents from her in court today, that they need additional documents and time for discovery. Ms. Travaglio stated that they would like to keep this informal and that they have hundreds of hours of video of Ms. Anello telling her stories. Mr. Leviloff stated that they have the right to discovery, that if they have to resort to formal discovery it will take time, that they are trying to determine what went on, that there is a lien on the property, that a transfer took place and that beyond the issues of competency are issues of undue influence. Ms. Travaglio stated that the decedent lived alone, that they have provided counsel with a witness list of 22 people who can attest to her competency and that prior to the will they are fighting about was done the grandkids stood to get nothing. Mr. Leviloff stated that he does not think that is correct. The Court stated that it will set a status for July 25, 2016, that if there is no substantial progress at that time it will set the matter for mediation within 60 days and that it hopes the informal discovery sheds light for everyone. The Petition for Probate of Will and Letters Testamentary was continued to July 25, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Boncato. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jun-13-2016 Continued To Jun-27-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Chris Anello and Michelle Forest.

Mr. Anello stated that he desired a continuance in order to file an objection to the petition. The matter was continued to June 27, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Kevin Macksoud. (Not reported)
